source_paper_title: Exactness and Effective Degree Bound of Lasserre’s Relaxation for Polynomial Optimization over Finite Variety
context:
Section 8 (Conclusion and perspectives), page 21 of the parsed text (paper page 21 / 29)

However, Assumption 1 is stronger than the regular sequence property of \(\{\bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_n\}\), see Section C. Indeed, \(\{\bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_n\}\) is still a regular sequence if \(\{g^\infty_1,\dots,g^\infty_n\}\) has finitely many complex common zero locus. However, the H-basis property may fail if we only assume that \(\{\bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_n\}\) is a regular sequence. It would be interesting to investigate the degree bound under the regular sequence condition only.
title: Effective degree bounds for exactness of Lasserre’s hierarchy under the regular-sequence condition without Assumption 1
latex:
Given integers \(n\ge 1\), \(k\ge n\), \(m\ge 0\), and polynomials \(f,g_1,\dots,g_k,h_1,\dots,h_m\in\mathbb{R}[x_1,\dots,x_n]\). Consider the polynomial optimization problem
\[
\min_{x\in\mathbb{R}^n}\ \ f(x)\quad\text{s.t.}\quad g_1(x)=\cdots=g_k(x)=0,\ \ h_1(x)\ge 0,\dots,h_m(x)\ge 0,
\]
and assume that the complex variety \(V_\mathbb{C}(g_1,\dots,g_k):=\{z\in\mathbb{C}^n: g_1(z)=\cdots=g_k(z)=0\}\) is finite and the feasible set is nonempty with optimal value \(f^*\in\mathbb{R}\).

For a polynomial \(p\in\mathbb{R}[x_1,\dots,x_n]\), define its homogenization \(\bar p\in\mathbb{R}[x_0,x_1,\dots,x_n]\) by
\[
\bar p(x_0,x_1,\dots,x_n)=x_0^{\deg(p)}\,p\Big(\frac{x_1}{x_0},\dots,\frac{x_n}{x_0}\Big),
\]
and let \(\bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_n\) be the homogenizations of \(g_1,\dots,g_n\) (the first \(n\) equality constraints). Let \(S:=\mathbb{R}[x_0,x_1,\dots,x_n]\).

Define the (untruncated) cone
\[
Q:=\langle g_1,\dots,g_k\rangle + \mathrm{QM}(1,h_1,\dots,h_m),
\]
where \(\langle g_1,\dots,g_k\rangle\) is the ideal generated by the equalities and \(\mathrm{QM}(1,h_1,\dots,h_m)\) is the quadratic module generated by \(1,h_1,\dots,h_m\). For an integer \(d\ge 0\), let \(Q_{2d}\) denote the standard truncation used in Lasserre’s hierarchy (i.e., sums \(\sum_i \lambda_i g_i + \sum_{j=0}^m \sigma_j h_j\) with \(\deg(\lambda_i g_i)\le 2d\), \(\deg(\sigma_j h_j)\le 2d\), and \(\sigma_j\) sums of squares).

Assume the weaker geometric condition that \(\bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_n\) form a regular sequence in \(S\) (equivalently, the homogeneous ideal \(\langle \bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_n\rangle\subset S\) has codimension \(n\)), but do not assume Assumption 1 of the paper (i.e., do not assume that the homogeneous leading parts \(g_1^\infty,\dots,g_n^\infty\) have no common nonzero complex root).

Problem: Determine an explicit, effective upper bound \(d\) (as a function of \(\deg(f)\), \(\deg(g_i)\), \(\deg(h_j)\), and possibly \(n,k,m\)) such that the exactness certificate
\[
 f-f^*\in Q_{2d}
\]
holds whenever it holds in \(Q\) (equivalently, whenever Lasserre’s hierarchy is exact at some finite level), under the sole assumption that \(\bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_n\) form a regular sequence (and appropriate standard conditions ensuring finite convergence/exactness), but without assuming the stronger “no solutions at infinity” condition.
location_in_paper: Section 8 (Conclusion and perspectives), page 21 of the parsed text (paper page 21 / 29)
open_problem_quote: However, Assumption 1 is stronger than the regular sequence property of \(\{\bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_n\}\), see Section C. Indeed, \(\{\bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_n\}\) is still a regular sequence if \(\{g^\infty_1,\dots,g^\infty_n\}\) has finitely many complex common zero locus. However, the H-basis property may fail if we only assume that \(\{\bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_n\}\) is a regular sequence. It would be interesting to investigate the degree bound under the regular sequence condition only.
